articles.title: Importing Electric Vehicles to Europe: Regulations, Taxes, and Best Routes
articles.summary: Comprehensive guide to importing electric vehicles into Europe, covering EU regulations, incentives, port selection, and logistics planning for business fleets.
articles.content:
Electric vehicles (EVs) are increasingly popular in European fleets due to strict emissions targets and expanding low-emission zones. This guide explains how to import electric vehicles to Europe from major sourcing markets such as Japan and the United Kingdom.

1. Regulatory overview
- EU-wide type approval requirements for EVs
- Battery safety standards and UN ECE regulations
- Differences between EU member states (e.g. Germany, Netherlands, Poland, Czech Republic)

2. Customs and taxes
- Import duties on electric vehicles by country
- VAT treatment for business vs. private buyers
- How reduced or zero-emission tax incentives work in selected European markets

3. Choosing ports and routes
- Common discharge ports for EV imports: Rotterdam, Bremerhaven, Antwerp, Gdansk, Koper
- When to choose Ro-Ro vs. container shipping for electric cars
- Special handling requirements for high-voltage batteries, including state-of-charge limits

4. Documentation checklist
- Commercial invoice and purchase agreement
- Certificate of origin and export certificate
- Battery compliance documents and MSDS where required

5. Risk management for corporate importers
- Marine insurance with EV-specific clauses
- Carrier selection with proven EV handling experience
- Building a phased rollout plan for fleet electrification across multiple European countries.

Use this guide as a baseline and consult our import specialists for country-specific advice when planning your next electric vehicle import program into Europe.

articles.title: How to Import Used Cars from Japan: Step-by-Step Process
articles.summary: Step-by-step walkthrough of sourcing, inspecting, buying, and shipping used vehicles from Japan to Africa, Europe, and the Middle East.
articles.content:
Japan remains one of the most popular sourcing markets for used vehicles thanks to high maintenance standards and a large selection of hybrid and diesel options.

1. Finding the right vehicle
- Using online inventory filters by body type, fuel type, and price
- Verifying auction inspection sheets and mileage

2. Purchase and documentation
- Pro forma invoice and payment terms
- Export certificate and deregistration

3. Shipping options
- Ro-Ro vs. container for sedans, SUVs, and hatchbacks
- Typical transit times to Mombasa, Tema, Lagos, Jebel Ali, and European ports

4. Import duties and compliance
- Overview for key destinations: Kenya, Ghana, Nigeria, Poland, Czech Republic, UAE
- Age restrictions and emissions requirements

5. Corporate buyers
- Consolidated shipping for fleets
- Using import cost calculators and fleet proposal tools on our website.

Work closely with our team to align vehicle selection, shipping schedules, and import regulations for your target markets.

articles.title: Planning a Corporate Fleet in Eastern Europe: Sedans, SUVs, and EVs
articles.summary: Guidance for corporate clients building fleets in Poland, Czech Republic, and neighboring markets, with budgeting tips and vehicle mix recommendations.
articles.content:
Eastern Europe is an increasingly attractive region for corporate fleet deployment. Countries such as Poland and the Czech Republic offer strong logistics hubs, competitive operating costs, and improving EV infrastructure.

1. Choosing the right vehicle mix
- Mid-size sedans for sales and administration
- SUVs for executive and field operations
- Gradual introduction of electric vehicles for city fleets

2. Budgeting and lifecycle costs
- Setting per-vehicle budget limits (e.g. USD 22,000 for mid-size sedans)
- Residual value considerations for Japanese and European imports

3. Sourcing strategy
- Combining Japanese sedans with German hatchbacks and SUVs
- Leveraging Ro-Ro shipping to Baltic and North Sea ports

4. Working with fleet solutions providers
- Using online fleet proposal forms
- Aligning delivery schedules with shipping timetables and local registration processes.

Our Fleet Solutions team can create tailored multi-country proposals for Eastern Europe, including Poland and Czech Republic, with transparent cost breakdowns.

articles.title: New Shipping Schedule for UK to UAE Electric Vehicle Sailings
articles.summary: Updated sailing dates and booking guidelines for electric vehicle shipments from the United Kingdom to the United Arab Emirates.
articles.content:
We have updated our shipping schedule for electric vehicle (EV) shipments from the United Kingdom to the United Arab Emirates to reflect new carrier capacity and port rotations.

Key details:
- Dedicated EV-capable sailings from major UK ports to Jebel Ali
- Temperature-controlled decks and trained staff for high-voltage vehicles
- Cut-off times for delivery to port prior to sailing

How to use the schedule:
1. Navigate to the Shipping Schedule section on our website
2. Filter by origin country "United Kingdom" and destination country "United Arab Emirates"
3. Select "Electric" as vehicle type to view eligible sailings only
4. Choose a sailing date that matches your project timeline (e.g. 10th–20th of next month)
5. Submit a booking request for the number of vehicles you intend to ship.

For corporate clients consolidating multiple EV units, our team can help reserve block space and coordinate inland transport in both origin and destination countries.
